display_text: As a deployer of a high-risk AI system, does the system make decisions or assessments about natural persons in a public-body context that warrants a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA)?
hint_text: Article 27 requires FRIA only from: (a) bodies governed by public law (public authorities, agencies), or (b) private entities providing public services (healthcare, education, social services, housing, justice). One important exception: even public bodies are exempt from FRIA when the high-risk AI system is used in critical infrastructure. If you are a private commercial deployer (not providing public services), FRIA does not apply. Exception: the FRIA obligation does not apply to high-risk AI systems intended for use in critical infrastructure. Source: Article 27(1).

options.label: Yes — I am a public body (or perform public functions) deploying a high-risk AI system that assesses or affects natural persons
Yes — I am a public body, but the system is intended for use in critical infrastructure
No — I am not a public body, or the system does not require an FRIA in my deployment context
